63ab959c491bf8105af0ce77.question: This political figure was instrumental in the creation of the planned community of Arthurdale in West Virginia, meant to be a model community for impoverished coal miners. From 1935 till her death in 1962, she wrote a syndicated daily newspaper column titled “My Day.” She resigned from the Daughters of the American Revolution when they denied the use of Constitution Hall for a concert by African-American singer Marian Anderson, arranging for Anderson to sing at the Lincoln Memorial. After her husband’s death, she was appointed to the UN General Assembly by Harry Truman. For 10 points name this first lady whose husband was elected US President four times.
63ab959c491bf8105af0ce77.question_sanitized: This political figure was instrumental in the creation of the planned community of Arthurdale in West Virginia, meant to be a model community for impoverished coal miners. From 1935 till her death in 1962, she wrote a syndicated daily newspaper column titled "My Day." She resigned from the Daughters of the American Revolution when they denied the use of Constitution Hall for a concert by African-American singer Marian Anderson, arranging for Anderson to sing at the Lincoln Memorial. After her husband's death, she was appointed to the UN General Assembly by Harry Truman. For 10 points name this first lady whose husband was elected US President four times.

63ab959c491bf8105af0ce85.question: An opera by a composer with this name contains the "Chorus of Exiled Palestinians" and the murder of the wheelchair-bound title character. In a musical, a character with this name sings "We piddle, twiddle, and resolve / Not one damn thing do we solve." That character with this name sings "I say vote yes" and "Vote for independency" in a song in which others repeat "Someone oughta open up a window." The composer with this name also wrote an opera in which a U.S. president visits an Asian country and Chairman Mao dances a foxtrot with his wife. For 10 points, identify this name shared by the composer of Nixon in China and a character from 1776 who is the subject of the song "Sit Down, John."

For 10 points, identify this name shared by the composer of Nixon in China and a character from 1776 who is the subject of the song "Sit Down, John."

63ab959c491bf8105af0ceb5.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about St. Peter's Basilica, for 10 points each.
parts: Pope Julius II commissioned this architect to design St. Peter's Basilica, and his other works include the circular Tempietto, which commemorated the site of St. Peter's crucifixion.
This man designed the dome of St. Peter's and also painted the Sistine Chapel's ceiling.
This man mostly completed the design of St. Peter's, which contains his canopy known as the baldacchino. Another of his works depicts an angel thrusting an arrow into Saint Theresa.

This man mostly completed the design of St. Peter's, which contains his canopy known as the baldacchino. Another of his works depicts an angel thrusting an arrow into Saint Theresa.

63ab959c491bf8105af0cefd.question: Cotton and Murillo discovered a ditungsten paddlewheel complex with an extremely low value of this quantity, which equals the negative energy of the HOMO, according to Koopmans' theorem. Calculating the enthalpy of formation of sodium chloride requires knowing this quantity for gaseous sodium. Mulliken electronegativity is the average of electron affinity and this quantity, which generally increases from left to right and decreases from top to bottom on the periodic table. For 10 points, name this energy required to remove the outermost electron from an atom.
63ab959c491bf8105af0cefd.question_sanitized: Cotton and Murillo discovered a ditungsten paddlewheel complex with an extremely low value of this quantity, which equals the negative energy of the HOMO, according to Koopmans' theorem. Calculating the enthalpy of formation of sodium chloride requires knowing this quantity for gaseous sodium. Mulliken electronegativity is the average of electron affinity and this quantity, which generally increases from left to right and decreases from top to bottom on the periodic table. For 10 points, name this energy required to remove the outermost electron from an atom.
63ab959c491bf8105af0cefd.answer_sanitized: ionization energy [or ionization potential; or IE; or IP]

63ab959c491bf8105af0cfca.question: This man accompanied photographer Jacob Riis on night-time walking tours of New York City slums in order to find sleeping officers while serving as Police Commissioner. He disparaged the tactics of Oliver Hazard Perry during the Battle of Lake Erie in his historical monograph titled The Naval War of 1812. In his greatest office, he directed the Great White Fleet of the U.S. Navy to make a two-year circumnavigation of the globe. He served four years without a vice president before defeating Alton Parker to win re-election, then in his second term won the Nobel Peace Prize for his role in ending the Russo-Japanese War. For 10 points, who is this 26th President of the United States who took office when William McKinley was assassinated?

In his greatest office, he directed the Great White Fleet of the U.S. Navy to make a two-year circumnavigation of the globe. He served four years without a vice president before defeating Alton Parker to win re-election, then in his second term won the Nobel Peace Prize for his role in ending the Russo-Japanese War. For 10 points, who is this 26th President of the United States who took office when William McKinley was assassinated?
